details: The “Mukhyamantri Matsya Sampada Yojana” was introduced by the Department of Fisheries, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to enhance fish production, generate employment opportunities, and improve the livelihoods of fish farmers by providing free training, field visits, and seminars on various fisheries-related activities.
benefits: Under this scheme, assistance is provided to small and large farmers/women engaged in various activities or items related to the fisheries sector, as mentioned in the table below
For a maximum of 2 years as per departmental standards as determined by scientific method of fisheries.
Maximum 2 units per individual and 4 or 6 units per committee/group etc.
Establishment of Small RAS Unit (1 tank of 50 sq. meter) / Biofloc Culture System (3 tanks of 4-meter diameter & 1.5-meter height)
Maximum 2 units for individuals or 4 units for committee/group etc.
Facility will be allowed only once for development of 1 beat
Interest Subvention for FIDF Scheme of Central Government
Interest subvention up to 2% on the approved item wise actual cost under FIDF Scheme of Central Government
Solar Power Support System for Aquaculture
Maximum 1 unit per individual or committee/group etc.
Matsya Saheli (for women or their committee/group)
Maximum 1 unit per beneficiary per block
Cluster based fish farming in hilly areas (Pond Construction)
Maximum 500 cubic meter for Individual and 1000 cubic meter for committee/group, etc.
Maximum 2 hectares for individual and 10 hectares for committee/group etc.
Maximum 2 units for individual and 5 units for committee/group etc.
Improvement of private/village community ponds in the plain area
Maximum 2 hectares for individual and 5 hectares for committee/group/lease pond etc.
Maximum 1 unit for individual, 2 units for lease pond and 4 units for committee/group etc.
As per market rate (decided by departmental committee)
As per departmental standards determined by scientific method of fisheries
Other input materials (nets, handnets, hapas, minikits)
Maximum 1 unit for individual and 2 units for committee/group etc.
Training, field visits, and seminars are provided to fish farmers free of cost.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
The applicant should have availability of land and a water supply source or should already be engaged in fish farming.
All individuals/applicants belonging to any caste category in Uttarakhand are eligible to avail the benefits under this scheme.

offline: To avail the benefits of the scheme, eligible beneficiaries must submit the application form either at the office of the Assistant Director of Fisheries / District Fisheries In-Charge at the district level or online through the
The application form must be accompanied by the Aadhaar card, land Khasra-Khatauni, permanent residence certificate, caste certificate, bank account details, and mobile number.
Beneficiaries under the scheme will be selected on a priority basis as per the guidelines of the scheme, following a first-come, first-served basis.

After submitting the application, a survey is conducted by fisheries inspectors or field personnel, and a proposal is prepared in collaboration with the beneficiary. Once the proposal is finalized, it is approved by the relevant district officer. Field personnel then supervise the work and provide technical assistance to ensure its completion, after which the subsidy is directly credited to the beneficiary’s account via DBT.

faqs.question: What are the basic eligibility criteria?
faqs.answer: Applicants must be residents of Uttarakhand and either have access to land and a water source or already be engaged in fish farming.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applications can be submitted at the office of the Assistant Director of Fisheries / District Fisheries In-Charge at the district level or online through the Apunni Sarkar portal.

faqs.question: How are beneficiaries selected?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries are selected based on priority according to the scheme’s guidelines on a first-come, first-served basis.

faqs.question: What happens after the application is submitted?
faqs.answer: Fisheries inspectors or field personnel conduct a survey and prepare a proposal in collaboration with the beneficiary.

faqs.question: Who approves the project proposal?
faqs.answer: The relevant district officer approves the proposal after it is prepared by field personnel and the beneficiary.

faqs.question: How is the subsidy disbursed?
faqs.answer: The subsidy is directly credited to the beneficiary’s bank account via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) after project completion.
